input.prompt:
Implement LEVER 4 (upstream journal/English source routing) in /home/polaris/wt/faithoff on branch fix/race-batch1-evidence-substrate, alongside uncommitted Levers 1-3. This is the HARDEST lever and touches evidence selection near the faithfulness boundary — be conservative and SAFE-BY-CONSTRUCTION. Central-config-gated PG_SOURCE_ROUTING (behavioral name, no adjective), default '' = byte-identical. Faithfulness is sacrosanct.

CONTEXT: the task 72 RQ says "only high-quality, English-language journal articles" but the report is grounded in working papers / IGO / gov / think-tank sources, and the prose even says "the working-paper version...". A source-eligibility firewall already EXISTS — study and WIRE it, do not rebuild:
- src/polaris_graph/retrieval/rq_eligibility.py — parses source_types/languages/recency from the RQ (via constraint_extractor) and demotes-and-retains ineligible sources. `_row_language` (~153) reads a sidecar only and FAILS OPEN (unknown => assumed eligible) with NO detection.
- src/polaris_graph/retrieval/quality_eligibility.py, document_type_classifier.py, evidence_selector.py — tier/kind/selection machinery.
- src/polaris_graph/planning/citation_reanchor.py (~90, ~261) — re-anchoring requires literal-span equivalence and its notion of "primary" ADMITS preprints/working papers, so it is NOT a journal check by itself (Sol's warning: never treat "primary" as "journal").

BUILD (three safe parts; all gated on PG_SOURCE_ROUTING, all default-off byte-identical):
1. LANGUAGE DETECTION (closes the English fail-open): add a lightweight, offline language check so an unknown-language row is DETECTED (e.g. script/stopword heuristic on the row's title/snippet — NO network) instead of assumed-English. Feed it into `_row_language` as a fallback ONLY when the sidecar is empty. When the RQ has an English constraint, a confidently-non-English row is demoted (the existing demote-and-retain path), NOT deleted. Default-off => `_row_language` unchanged.
2. JOURNAL-SOURCE PREFERENCE in selection: when the RQ constraint demands journal articles, bias evidence SELECTION/ranking toward peer-reviewed-journal-tier sources (using the existing tier/kind classifier — host/DOI/venue-driven, NOT a hardcoded venue list), so the report is grounded in journals from the start. This REORDERS preference; it must NOT drop a claim's only support (demote-and-retain stays; never strand a sentence uncited).
3. SAME-WORK JOURNAL-PREFERRED citation (safe subset only): when a claim is already supported by BOTH a grey row AND a peer-reviewed-journal row of the SAME work IN THE CORPUS (same-work identity already exists — see same_work_groups / origin cluster machinery), prefer the journal row for the inline cite. CRITICAL SAFETY (Sol): do NOT redirect on title match; only when same-work identity is already established AND the journal row's span supports the same claim — and RE-RUN strict verification on the re-pointed claim. If no in-corpus journal version exists, KEEP the grey cite (never strand, never fabricate, never acquire new sources in this fork — full external acquisition is OUT OF SCOPE, document it).

NO-ROLLBACK GATE (assert in code + test): the set of unique supported claims after routing must be >= baseline. If routing would strand or drop a claim, it must fall back to the grey cite instead. A test must prove no claim loses all support.

FAITHFULNESS: do NOT weaken strict_verify/provenance/is_verified. Part 3 re-points a citation ONLY with re-verification; if re-verify fails, keep the original. Language/preference parts operate on retrieval/selection metadata, upstream of composition, and never force an unverified claim into prose.

GATING/GENERALITY: flag PG_SOURCE_ROUTING default '' in config_defaults.py; language detection generic (any language, any RQ); journal classification host/DOI/venue-driven (no task-72 venue list). Off => byte-identical.

TESTS (tests/polaris_graph/test_lever4_source_routing.py):
- OFF byte-identity: eligibility/selection identical to today.
- Language detection: a clearly non-English title is detected and (with an English RQ constraint, flag on) demoted-not-deleted; an English title stays; unknown stays fail-open ONLY when detection is not confident.
- Journal preference: given a journal row + a grey row for different claims, journal ranks higher; a claim with ONLY grey support is NEVER dropped.
- Same-work journal-preferred: a claim with same-work journal+grey rows re-points to journal AND passes re-verify; if re-verify fails, keeps grey; a claim with only grey keeps grey.
- No-rollback: assert unique-supported-claim count after >= before on a mixed fixture.
Run: /home/polaris/conda_cu128/bin/python -m pytest tests/polaris_graph/test_lever4_source_routing.py -x -q plus retrieval/eligibility regression (any test_rq_eligibility / test_quality_eligibility / evidence_selector tests). Iterate to green. Import smoke.

Do NOT commit. Do NOT run RACE. Return: files+lines changed, the exact language-detection method (prove offline+general), how journal-tier is decided (prove host/DOI-driven, not a venue list), the same-work re-verify path (prove no title-match swap, re-verify enforced), the no-rollback proof, pytest results, and anything risky for the integrated Sol gate. If any part cannot be done safely in scope, implement the safe parts and CLEARLY flag what you deferred and why.

content.text:
<fork-boilerplate>
You are a worker fork. The transcript above is the parent's history — inherited reference, not your situation. You are NOT a continuation of that agent. Execute ONE directive, then stop.

Hard rules:
- Do NOT spawn subagents with the Agent tool. The "default to forking" guidance is for the parent; you ARE the fork, execute directly.
- One shot: report once and stop. No follow-up questions, no proposed next steps, no waiting for the user.

Guidelines (your directive may override any of these):
- Stay in scope. Other forks may be handling adjacent work; if you spot something outside your directive, note it in a sentence and move on.
- Open with one line restating your task, so the parent can spot scope drift at a glance.
- Be concise — as short as the answer allows, no shorter. Plain text, no preamble, no meta-commentary.
- If you committed changes, list the paths and commit hashes in your report.
</fork-boilerplate>

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch
